title: Redirect Semantics, Method Rewriting, Credential Scope, and Loop Control · Browser and Node… · Type-System Trace: Redirect Status Semantics
summary: A sign-in flow follows relative and cross-origin redirects while Fetch redirect modes, credentials, methods, and authorization scope differ. Planned artifact: Add and verify the http ts redirect status semantics increment in a TypeScript 7 standards-based Fetch auditable redirect policy engine. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Differentiate 301, 302, 303, 307, and 308 cache, permanence, method, and body consequences. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.

Reject unintended HTTP downgrades, insecure redirects, mixed content, and credential forwarding across weaker channels. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
